title: hepetis burchellii
intro: Hepetis burchellii, also known as Buchu, is a small, perennial plant native to South Africa that belongs to the Rutaceae family. The leaves of the plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Buchu include treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and digestive issues, as well as providing relief from fever and rheumatic pain. Buchu is often prepared in herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a cream or oil to treat skin conditions and wounds.
